name: daisyUI
description: The most popular, free and open-source Tailwind CSS component library with pure CSS components

description.value: The most popular, free and open-source Tailwind CSS component library with pure CSS components

initial-release.comment: Repository created November 28, 2020. Version 1.0 released in 2021 after initial development with version 0.x in late 2020.

framework.comment: Framework-agnostic Tailwind CSS plugin. Works with 40+ frameworks including React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, Solid, Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Astro, Laravel, Rails, Django, and more. Pure CSS with no JavaScript dependencies.

styling-approach.comment: Tailwind CSS plugin that adds semantic component class names (btn, card, modal, etc.) to Tailwind CSS. Pure CSS implementation with no JavaScript runtime.

component-model.comment: Provides pre-styled components with semantic class names. Components are styled CSS classes that can be customized using Tailwind utilities. Uses CSS-only techniques like checkbox hack for interactive states. No headless option - components ship with visual styling.

typescript.comment: N/A - daisyUI is a CSS-only library (Tailwind CSS plugin) with no JavaScript runtime, so TypeScript support is not applicable. It can be used in TypeScript projects without issues.

ssr-compatible.comment: Fully SSR compatible. Works with Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Remix, Astro, and other SSR frameworks. Being pure CSS with no JavaScript dependencies ensures perfect SSR compatibility.

rsc-compatible.comment: Compatible with React Server Components. Pure CSS approach means no client-side JavaScript is required for styling, making it ideal for RSC environments.

design-system.comment: Custom design system with 35 built-in themes and comprehensive theming capabilities. Not based on Material, Ant, or other existing design systems.

dark-mode.comment: Built-in dark mode support through theme system. Multiple dark themes available (dark, night, dracula, business, forest, halloween, synthwave, etc.). Can set preferred dark theme with --prefersdark flag.

theming-system.comment: Comprehensive theming system with 35 built-in themes. Supports custom theme creation, theme modification, and multiple simultaneous themes. Theme switching via data-theme attribute.

css-variables.comment: Extensive use of CSS custom properties for theming. Includes variables for colors (base, primary, secondary, accent, neutral, semantic colors), border radius, sizing, and visual effects.

design-tokens.comment: Fully tokenized design system with configurable tokens for colors, typography, spacing, border radius, and other design properties. Tokens can be customized when creating custom themes.

rtl-support.comment: Full RTL support since version 4. Uses CSS logical properties for automatic bidirectional support. Enable by adding dir='rtl' to HTML element - no configuration needed. Works at runtime without build-time configuration.

component-count.comment: 65 components across 6 main categories: Actions (6), Data Display (18), Navigation (8), Feedback (7), Data Input (14), Layout (8), plus Mockup category (4)

text-input.comment: Text Input component with variants for different states (bordered, ghost, primary, secondary, accent, info, success, warning, error) and sizes (xs, sm, md, lg)

textarea.comment: Textarea component for multi-line text input with same size and color variants as text input

select.comment: Select dropdown component with multiple styling variants

multi-select.comment: No dedicated multi-select component. Would need to be built using native HTML select with multiple attribute or custom JavaScript.

combobox.comment: No built-in combobox/autocomplete component. Basic select is available but not searchable/filterable without custom JavaScript.

checkbox.comment: Checkbox component with multiple color variants and sizes. Pure CSS styling of native checkbox inputs.

radio.comment: Radio button component with color variants and sizes. Pure CSS styling of native radio inputs.

switch-toggle.comment: Toggle component styled to look like a switch button. Based on native checkbox input with pure CSS styling.

slider.comment: Range slider component with color variants and sizes. Single thumb slider based on native range input. No dual-thumb range support.

number-input.comment: No dedicated number input with increment/decrement buttons. Can use text input with type='number' but without styled steppers.

tag-input.comment: No built-in tag/token input component. Has Badge component that could be used to build tag input with custom JavaScript.

pin-input.comment: No dedicated OTP/PIN code input component

color-picker.comment: No color picker component beyond basic HTML input type='color'

date-picker.comment: Has Calendar component with styles for calendar libraries, but no built-in date picker functionality. Provides styling only - requires integration with date libraries.

file-upload.comment: File Input component for uploading files with styled native file input. No preview functionality built-in.

rating-input.comment: Rating component using radio inputs styled as stars or other symbols. Pure CSS implementation with customizable symbols.

input-groups.comment: Join component allows grouping inputs with buttons, text, or other elements. Can combine inputs with addons.

input-with-dropdown.comment: Can be built using Join and Dropdown components together to create input with attached dropdown

button-groups.comment: Join component can group buttons together as segmented controls or button groups

split-button.comment: Can be built by combining Button and Dropdown components, though not a dedicated split button component

table.comment: Basic table component with styling variants (zebra striping, sizes from xs to xl), pinned rows/columns for sticky headers

data-table.comment: No advanced data table with built-in sorting, filtering, or pagination. Basic table styling only - advanced features require custom JavaScript or external libraries.

virtual-infinite-list.comment: No virtualized list component for large datasets

tree-view.comment: No tree view component. Could potentially be built using nested Collapse components with custom implementation.

list-view.comment: List component for styled lists with item components

card.comment: Card component for grouping and displaying content with multiple styling options

avatar.comment: Avatar component with fallback support, multiple sizes, shapes (circle, square), and group/stack layouts

badge.comment: Badge component for status indicators with color variants and sizes. Can be placed on other elements.

tag-chip-pill.comment: Badge component serves as tag/chip/pill. Supports different styles and can be made removable with custom JavaScript.

timeline.comment: Timeline component showing events in chronological order. Supports both horizontal and vertical orientations with icons and custom content.

calendar.comment: Calendar component provides styles for different calendar libraries but is not a full calendar implementation. Display only - requires JavaScript library integration for functionality.

alert.comment: Alert component for static banner messages with color variants (info, success, warning, error)

toast-notification.comment: Toast component for temporary popup notifications. Positioning handled by CSS - requires JavaScript for show/hide/dismiss logic.

progress-bar.comment: Progress bar component for determinate progress indicators with color variants

progress-circle.comment: Radial progress component for circular progress indicators with customizable size and colors

spinner-loader.comment: Loading component with multiple styles (spinner, dots, ring, ball, bars, infinity) for indeterminate loading indicators

skeleton.comment: Skeleton component for content placeholders during loading

empty-state.comment: No dedicated empty state component. Can be built using Card, Typography, and other components.

modal-dialog.comment: Modal/Dialog component with 3 implementation methods: HTML dialog element (recommended, requires JS), checkbox-based (CSS-only), and anchor links (CSS-only). Supports ESC key closure with native dialog.

drawer-sheet.comment: Drawer component for slide-in panels from left or right side. Grid layout that can show/hide sidebar based on screen size or checkbox toggle.

popover.comment: No dedicated popover component. Dropdown can serve some popover use cases but is not identical.

tooltip.comment: Tooltip component for hover hint text with multiple positions (top, bottom, left, right) and color variants. Note: Does not fully comply with WCAG 2.2 criteria 1.4.13 for dismissible and hoverable requirements.

dropdown-menu.comment: Dropdown component with 3 implementation methods: details/summary (native HTML, CSS-only), Popover API with anchor positioning (modern, limited browser support), and CSS focus (pure CSS). No JavaScript required for basic functionality.

context-menu.comment: No dedicated right-click context menu component

tabs.comment: Tabs component for tab-based content switching with multiple styling options (bordered, lifted, boxed)

vertical-tabs.comment: No vertical tabs orientation support. Tabs are horizontal only.

breadcrumb.comment: Breadcrumbs component for hierarchical path navigation with icon support and max-width scrolling

pagination.comment: Pagination component for page navigation controls using Join and Button components

stepper.comment: Steps component for multi-step process indicator. Supports horizontal and vertical layouts with customizable step content and colors. Visual indicator only - navigation logic requires custom implementation.

navigation-menu.comment: Menu component for site navigation with support for icons, nested menus, and collapsible sections

sidebar.comment: Drawer component serves as collapsible sidebar navigation. Can be visible by default on large screens (lg:drawer-open). Complex collapsible behavior requires custom JavaScript.

bottom-navigation.comment: Bottom navigation component for mobile-style bottom nav bar. Updated to use aria-disabled instead of disabled class for better accessibility.

grid.comment: No dedicated Grid component. Uses Tailwind CSS grid utilities for grid layouts.

stack.comment: Stack component for positioning elements on top of each other

container.comment: No dedicated Container component. Uses Tailwind CSS container utility for centered max-width containers.

divider.comment: Divider component for horizontal and vertical separators with optional text

aspect-ratio.comment: No dedicated Aspect Ratio component. Uses Tailwind CSS aspect-ratio utilities.

collapsible.comment: Collapse component for expandable/collapsible content sections. Multiple implementations: focus-based, checkbox-based, and details/summary (native semantics).

rich-text-editors.comment: No official rich text editor integrations. Pure CSS library with no JavaScript dependencies, so rich text editor integration would be third-party only.

code-editors.comment: Has Mockup Code component for displaying code snippets, but no code editor integration. Pure CSS library.

chart-libraries.comment: No built-in or official chart integrations. Third-party chart libraries (ApexCharts, Chart.js) can integrate by using daisyUI CSS variables for colors.

form-libraries.comment: No official form library integrations. As a CSS-only library, daisyUI form components work with any form management library. Community React wrapper (react-daisyui) exists but is separate project.

animation-libraries.comment: Built-in CSS animations for Text Rotate (infinite loop animation, 10s duration) and Hover 3D (interactive 3D tilt effect). Pure CSS animations with no JavaScript dependencies. Compatible with animation libraries like Framer Motion for additional effects.

icon-libraries.comment: Documentation examples use Heroicons (v2) from Tailwind CSS ecosystem. Compatible with any icon library (Font Awesome, Lucide, Phosphor, etc.) as daisyUI is CSS-only and doesn't bundle icons.

date-libraries.comment: Calendar component provides styling for date libraries but doesn't include built-in date library. Compatible with popular date libraries (date-fns, dayjs, luxon) for calendar functionality.

aria-compliant.comment: Components use semantic HTML elements which are accessible by default (e.g., toggle is native checkbox). ARIA attributes and roles added to code examples in 2024. Uses native elements like checkbox, radio, details/summary for better accessibility vs div-based implementations.

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard support for interactive components. 2024 improvements added ability to focus on components using keyboard navigation. Uses semantic HTML for inherent keyboard accessibility.

screen-reader-tested.comment: Components tested with screen readers. Accessibility improvements in 2024 specifically addressed screen reader compatibility. Semantic HTML approach ensures better screen reader support than div-based alternatives.

focus-management.comment: Proper focus management in interactive components. Dropdowns and modals handle focus appropriately. Uses native HTML elements which provide focus management by default.

wcag-level.comment: All color pairs in built-in themes tested for contrast ratio and pass WCAG test. Automated testing runs on theme changes. Some components (like tooltips) don't fully meet WCAG 2.2 criteria 1.4.13.

accessibility-rating.comment: Rating: 4 (Good). Strong accessibility foundation with semantic HTML, WCAG AA color contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader testing, and focus management. ARIA attributes added to examples. Some limitations exist (tooltip WCAG 2.2 compliance, dropdown dismissal). Excellent for a CSS-only library, though not perfect across all WCAG 2.2 criteria.

bundle-size-full.comment: 34 KB compressed for all-inclusive CSS file (daisyui.css) with all components and colors in daisyUI 5. This is the CDN file size. 75% smaller than previous version (was 137 KB). Note: This is CSS bundle size, not JavaScript - daisyUI has zero JS runtime.

bundle-size-core.comment: Not applicable. daisyUI is a Tailwind CSS plugin that generates CSS at build time based on which components you use. Bundle size depends on which components and utilities are actually used in your project. Tree-shaking happens via Tailwind CSS's purge/content configuration.

tree-shakeable.comment: Supports tree-shaking via Tailwind CSS content configuration. Can use include/exclude options to selectively load specific components. Unused components are automatically purged by Tailwind CSS build process.

zero-runtime.comment: Pure CSS with no JavaScript runtime. All styling is compile-time only via Tailwind CSS plugin. No client-side JavaScript needed for styling. Interactive behaviors use CSS-only techniques (checkbox hack, :focus, details/summary) or native HTML.

lazy-loading.comment: Not applicable. Components are CSS classes compiled at build time by Tailwind CSS. All used component styles are included in final CSS bundle. No runtime component loading since there's no JavaScript.

documentation-quality.comment: Rating: 4 (Good). Comprehensive documentation with component examples, API reference, installation guides for 40+ frameworks, theming documentation, and accessibility notes. Interactive examples on component pages. 2024 improvements added notes and comments for better context. Missing: more advanced patterns/recipes, migration guides could be more detailed, some edge cases not fully documented.

interactive-docs.comment: Documentation includes interactive component examples that can be previewed directly on the page. Each component page shows live examples with code snippets.

storybook.comment: React wrapper (react-daisyui) has Storybook available at react.daisyui.com. Note: This is for the React wrapper, not the core daisyUI library itself which is CSS-only.

figma-kit.comment: Official daisyUI Figma Library available (commercial product, $49-$399). Includes all 61+ components, 2500+ variants, all theme colors, and design tokens. Free community preview available. Fully tokenized with variables for perfect design-to-code matching.

cli-generator.comment: CLI tool available via 'npm create daisyui' or 'npm init daisyui'. Installs Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, and daisyUI with automatic config setup. Multiple setup options for new or existing projects.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~508k weekly downloads as of December 2024. 13 million downloads in 2024 (2x growth from 2023). Used in 333,000+ open source projects.

active-maintenance.comment: Very active maintenance. 104 version releases in 2024 with 400+ bug fixes. Latest release v5.5.14 on December 14, 2024. Regular updates and community engagement. 299 contributors.

commercial-support.comment: No commercial support or enterprise tier. Free and open source (MIT license). Supported through Open Collective sponsorship model. Official paid Figma kit available but no paid support or licenses.

style-override.comment: Multiple override methods: Apply additional Tailwind utility classes (className), use CSS @apply directive in stylesheets, customize via CSS variables, modify theme tokens, use prefix config to avoid conflicts. No style-prop since it's CSS-only.

compound-components.comment: Not applicable. daisyUI is a CSS-only library providing class names, not JavaScript components. No component composition pattern like <Menu.Item> since there are no JS components.

render-props.comment: Not applicable. Pure CSS library with no JavaScript components, so render props pattern doesn't apply.

slots.comment: Not applicable. CSS-only library without JavaScript component architecture. Content structure is defined by HTML markup using daisyUI classes.

unstyled-mode.comment: No unstyled mode. daisyUI is a styled component library - the entire purpose is to provide pre-styled components via CSS classes. All components ship with visual styling.
